description: First three guide-style documentation pages for Bethel ChMS: Getting Started (intro, setup, invite), Roles & Permissions, and Recording Attendance

userStories.title: Remove template pages and set up docs structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to remove the Mintlify template pages and create the directory structure and docs.json navigation skeleton for the new documentation pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove quickstart.mdx and development.mdx from project root
Remove essentials/, api-reference/, ai-tools/, snippets/ directories
Remove all references to deleted pages from docs.json navigation
Create getting-started/ and guides/ directories
Add 'Getting started' and 'Guides' navigation groups to docs.json with placeholder paths for all five new pages
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This must run first so subsequent stories can add their pages to the existing navigation structure.

userStories.title: Write introduction page — What is Bethel?
userStories.description: As a prospective user landing on the docs, I want a one-sentence explanation of what Bethel is and who it's for, so I can confirm this product is relevant to me.
acceptanceCriteria: Create getting-started/introduction.mdx with title, description, and sidebarTitle frontmatter
Page opens with a single-sentence description of what Bethel is and who it's for
Brief bullet list of capabilities: manage members, track attendance, run events, handle visitors
A 'What you'll need' note callout: just an email address and a few minutes
Ends with a clear call-to-action linking to /getting-started/setup
Screenshot placeholder as MDX comment: {/* Screenshot: Bethel dashboard overview */}
Page path is listed in docs.json navigation under Getting started group
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write setup page — Set up your church
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a single walkthrough that takes me from sign-up to a fully provisioned branch, so I can start using Bethel immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Create getting-started/setup.mdx with title, description, and sidebarTitle frontmatter
Uses <Steps> component for the sequential flow
Step 1 — Sign up: email and password, validation rules (min 8 characters)
Step 2 — Create your organisation: explains what an organisation is on first use, lists form fields (Name required, Email required, Address optional, Post Code optional)
Info callout: 'You automatically become the organisation owner — the highest permission level in Bethel.'
Step 3 — Create your first branch: explains what a branch is on first use, lists form fields (Branch Name required, Location Name required, Sunday Service start/end time)
Tip callout explaining auto-provisioned items: primary location, Sunday Service event, 52 weekly occurrences
Step 4 — You're in: brief dashboard description, links to next steps (invite users or add members)
Screenshot placeholders as MDX comments for: sign-up form, create organisation form, create branch form, dashboard after setup
Page path is listed in docs.json navigation under Getting started group
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write invite users page — Invite your team
userStories.description: As an org owner who just completed setup, I want to invite my first team members and assign them the right roles, so we can start working together in Bethel.
acceptanceCriteria: Create getting-started/invite-users.mdx with title, description, and sidebarTitle frontmatter
Brief intro: 'Now that your church is set up, invite the people who'll help manage it.'
Uses <Steps> for the invitation flow
Step 1 — Open user management: where to find it in the UI
Step 2 — Send an invitation: form fields (Email required, Name required, Role required, Branch required if branch-scoped), explains magic link email
Step 3 — Choosing a role: plain-language summary of three invitable roles (org admin, branch admin, editor) with link to /guides/roles-and-permissions
Info callout: who can invite whom hierarchy (org owner → any; org admin → org admin/branch admin/editor; branch admin → branch admin/editor in own branch; editor → nobody)
Note callout: invitations expire after 7 days
Step 4 — What the invitee sees: accept invitation page, set password
Ends with 'What to do next' pointing to adding members
Screenshot placeholders as MDX comments for: invite user form with role dropdown, invitation email / accept invitation page
Page path is listed in docs.json navigation under Getting started group
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write roles and permissions page
userStories.description: As any Bethel user, I want to understand what each role can do, so I know my own capabilities and can assign roles appropriately to my team.
acceptanceCriteria: Create guides/roles-and-permissions.mdx with title, description, and sidebarTitle frontmatter
Opens with brief intro: Bethel has four roles, two scopes
The four roles section with plain-language descriptions: Organisation owner, Organisation admin, Branch admin, Editor
Org-scoped vs branch-scoped section explaining practical differences
Info callout explaining why org-level roles cannot edit branch records (branch admins own their data, headquarters has visibility not control)

Per-role capabilities section using Accordion panels covering: manage org settings, manage branches, manage users, manage members, manage events, manage locations, record attendance, manage visitors, delete records
Who can invite whom section with the invitation hierarchy
How role changes take effect section (JWT refresh, no logout needed)
Transferring ownership section (current owner only, describes flow)
Screenshot placeholders as MDX comments for: user list showing role badges, role selector in user edit form, transfer ownership confirmation dialog
Page path is listed in docs.json navigation under Guides group
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write recording attendance page
userStories.description: As a branch admin or editor, I want to understand all the ways I can record attendance in Bethel, so I can pick the method that works best for my church.
acceptanceCriteria: Create guides/recording-attendance.mdx with title, description, and sidebarTitle frontmatter
Opens with brief intro: several ways to record attendance, pick or mix and match
Selecting an event occurrence section: attendance recorded against a specific occurrence, how to navigate to it
Manual check-in section: search member/visitor by name, mark present
QR code check-in section: event QR code flow only (scan → enter email/mobile → identify → confirm/register → checked in). Do NOT mention member-level QR codes
Tip callout: print QR code and display at venue entrance
Self-service kiosk section: 3-5 sentence overview with Note callout linking to future dedicated guide
Admin entry after the event section: bulk entry for paper sign-in sheets
Viewing attendance section: three views (by event, by member, by date range)
Editing attendance section: edits are logged with who made the change
Members vs visitors section: both appear in records, visitor visit count auto-updates, conversion carries over history
Info callout: who can do this (Branch Admin, Editor can record/edit; Org Owner, Org Admin can view only)
Screenshot placeholders as MDX comments for: manual check-in, event QR code, public check-in screen, kiosk mode, bulk attendance entry, attendance list filtered by event
Page path is listed in docs.json navigation under Guides group
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: IMPORTANT: Do NOT mention members having their own QR codes. Only document the event QR code flow.

userStories.title: Create placeholder managing members page and validate
userStories.description: As a user following the Getting Started flow, I need the 'managing members' link to resolve so there are no broken links in the documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create guides/managing-members.mdx with title, description, sidebarTitle frontmatter and a 'Coming soon' body
Page path is listed in docs.json navigation under Guides group
All internal links across all pages use root-relative paths without file extensions
mint broken-links passes with no errors
mint validate passes with no errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the final validation story. Run mint broken-links and mint validate to confirm everything is wired up correctly.
